**Need help in using myTax portal?** Browse the [myTax Portal Technical FAQ](https://www.iras.gov.sg/digital-services/others/mytax-portal-technical-faq "myTax Portal Technical FAQ"). Singapore Corporate Access (or Corppass) is the sole authorisation system for entities to manage digital service access of employees or third party (e.g. tax agent) who need to perform corporate transactions with government agencies online. To access IRAS digital services on your behalf, your staff or tax agent must first be authorised via Corppass. For assistance on Corppass setup, please refer to the relevant [Step-by-Step Guides](https://www.iras.gov.sg/digital-services/others/corppass "Step-by-Step Guides"). title: IRAS | Singpass Foreign user Account (SFA) for Foreign Individuals
text:
**What is Singpass Foreign user Account (SFA)?**

A Singpass Foreign user Account (SFA) allows foreign users to access selected government and private sector digital services in Singapore, including personal and corporate tax services on myTax Portal.

**Who needs to apply for an SFA?**

a) You must apply for an SFA with IRAS if you are a foreign individual without a Singapore Government-issued ID, and you need to transact with us digitally for Individual and/or Property Tax matters. This includes:

\- Singaporeans/Singapore PRs who have renounced their citizenship/Permanent Residence

\- Former FIN holders who have cancelled their work pass

\- Foreigners with an existing IRAS Tax reference number (e.g., A1234567A)

For Corppass users (including Tax Agents) handling business tax matters, an SFA will be issued automatically by Corppass when you apply for a Corppass user account. In this case, there is no need to apply for an SFA separately with IRAS.

**How to Apply for an SFA?**

Complete the **[application](https://go.gov.sg/iras-sfa)** form to apply for an SFA. Before you begin, please ensure you have the following prepared:

a. Your National ID (e.g., Malaysian IC, National Identity Card). If unavailable, a passport document with at least 6 months validity.

b. A valid email address. Your SFA ID will be sent to this email upon successful processing.

      \- Ensure you can access the email inbox before proceeding.

      \- Verify that the email address has not been used for previous SFA applications or registered with Singpass.

c. Your existing/previous tax reference number with IRAS, if any (e.g., A1234567A, G1234567X, S1234567D).

The new SFA account has to be activated **within** 90 days from date of issuance. Otherwise, the account will be terminated by Singpass, and you will need to reapply for a new SFA account with IRAS.

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| ### **Steps to activate SFA** |

**Step 2:**
Download the Singpass mobile app and click on "Start here" to setup your SFA. Setup must be completed within **90 days** from the date of the email.
**Step 3:**
Congratulations!
Upon the setup of your SFA, you may login to myTax Portal the **next working day**. | # FAQs ## A. SFA Application Expand all [1\. I have renounced my Singapore citizenship/Permanent Residence or my FIN has expired. Which tax reference number should I use in the Singpass Foreign Account (SFA) application form?](https://www.iras.gov.sg/digital-services/others/singpass-foreign-user-account-(sfa)-for-foreign-individuals#1--i-have-renounced-my-singapore-citizenship-permanent-residence-or-my-fin-has-expired--which-tax-reference-number-should-i-use-in-the-singpass-foreign-account--sfa--application-form-) You should still enter the NRIC or FIN that was previously issued to you when filling out the application form, even if it is no longer valid. [2\. Can both my spouse and I use the same email address when applying for our Singpass Foreign User Accounts?](https://www.iras.gov.sg/digital-services/others/singpass-foreign-user-account-(sfa)-for-foreign-individuals#2--can-both-my-spouse-and-i-use-the-same-email-address-when-applying-for-our-

title: IRAS | Singpass Foreign user Account (SFA) for Foreign Individuals
text:
ser Accounts?](https://www.iras.gov.sg/digital-services/others/singpass-foreign-user-account-(sfa)-for-foreign-individuals#2--can-both-my-spouse-and-i-use-the-same-email-address-when-applying-for-our-singpass-foreign-user-accounts-)

No, each applicant must use a unique email address that was not used for Singpass or IRAS Unique Account when applying for their own Singpass Foreign Account (SFA). You and your spouse will need to provide separate email addresses for your individual applications.


[3\. What can I expect after submitting my SFA application?](https://www.iras.gov.sg/digital-services/others/singpass-foreign-user-account-(sfa)-for-foreign-individuals#3--what-can-i-expect-after-submitting-my-sfa-application-)

You can expect to receive the activation email within 10 working days from Singpass. Please do check your email’s junk or spam folder as well. If you still do not receive the activation email, you may [contact Singpass](https://www.singpass.gov.sg/main/) for assistance.


[4\. Do I have to re-apply for a SFA with IRAS to login myTax Portal if I have been issued with a SFA by another agency?](https://www.iras.gov.sg/digital-services/others/singpass-foreign-user-account-(sfa)-for-foreign-individuals#4--do-i-have-to-re-apply-for-a-sfa-with-iras-to-login-mytax-portal-if-i-have-been-issued-with-a-sfa-by-another-agency-)

SFA can be issued by different SFA organisations (e.g. Corppass). SFA can only be used to access the digital services of the SFA organisation that issued the account to you.

If you have a SFA ID issued by another SFA organisation and you wish to access IRASʼ digital services for Individual and/or Property Tax matters, please complete the application form and indicate your existing SFA ID (e.g. Y1234567A).

You also need to attach the same Foreign ID document (e.g. Passport document or National ID) that was submitted to the other SFA organisation.

## B. Unable to log in to myTax Portal

text:
reign ID document (e.g. Passport document or National ID) that was submitted to the other SFA organisation.

## B. Unable to log in to myTax Portal

Expand all

[1\. I do not recall my SFA User ID i.e., Y-series no.](https://www.iras.gov.sg/digital-services/others/singpass-foreign-user-account-(sfa)-for-foreign-individuals#1--i-do-not-recall-my-sfa-user-id-i-e---y-series-no-)

You may retrieve your SFA User ID (e.g., Y1234567C) [at this Singpass page](https://portal.singpass.gov.sg/home/ui/retrieve-account/sfa).


[2\. I am unable to access myTax Portal after setting up my SFA.](https://www.iras.gov.sg/digital-services/others/singpass-foreign-user-account-(sfa)-for-foreign-individuals#2--i-am-unable-to-access-mytax-portal-after-setting-up-my-sfa-)

Please allow **one working day** for the system to sync your details before logging in.


[3\. I am not able to log into myTax Portal using the issued SFA.](https://www.iras.gov.sg/digital-services/others/singpass-foreign-user-account-(sfa)-for-foreign-individuals#3--i-am-not-able-to-log-into-mytax-portal-using-the-issued-sfa-)

Please consider the following:

a. **SFA Activation:** Ensure that you have activated your SFA **within 90 days** from the date of issuance. Otherwise, the account will be terminated by Singpass and you will need to [resubmit](https://go.gov.sg/iras-sfa) a new application with us.

b. **Validity of Official Document:** Check that the official document used for your SFA application is not expired nor due to expire within the next six months. If it has expired or is nearing expiry, [submit](https://go.gov.sg/iras-sfa) a new application with your renewed document. When applying, select "Updating of expiring/expired official identification document(s)" as your Reason for Application.

title: Check Corporate Income Tax Statuses
text:
Our IRAS Bot can assist companies in checking the following statuses. Simply click on the “Chat with IRAS” icon at the bottom right of the screen and select the relevant options.

**Form C-S/ Form C-S (Lite)/ Form C (for the current and 3 previous Years of Assessment):**

- Filing Status
- Tax Assessment Status

**Applications/ Submissions:**

- Revise/ Object to Assessment
- Application for strike-off
- Certificate/ Letter of Residence
- Declaration of Receipts and Payments for Companies under Liquidation

**Corporate Income Tax Payment:**

- GIRO Status
- Payment Status

For our IRAS Bot to assist with your status check, please have the company’s tax reference number\* ready.

_\\* The company's tax reference number is its Unique Entity Number (UEN), which falls within the categories below:_

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| Local Companies with UEN | E.g. 200312345A |
| Foreign Companies with UEN | E.g. T08FC1234A |

title: IRAS | Digital services - Companies/ Tax agents
text:
File your Year of Assessment 2026 Corporate Income Tax Return before the 30 Nov 2026 deadline to avoid penalties.

For Tax Agents – To ensure smooth and accurate income tax filing submissions, please:

- Do not open multiple client filing windows at the same time when preparing and submitting Corporate Income Tax return on myTax Portal.

- Complete and submit each client's tax return before moving to the next.

Singapore Corporate Access (or Corppass) is the sole authorisation system for entities to manage digital service access of employees or third parties (e.g. tax agents) who need to perform corporate transactions with government agencies online. To access IRAS' digital services in an entity's myTax Portal account, employees or third parties (e.g. tax agents) must
first be authorised via Corppass.

An entity which has authorised its employees or third parties (e.g. tax agents) to access its myTax Portal account via Corppass will be deemed to have authorised its employees or third parties (e.g. tax agents) to:

- Handle its Corporate Income Tax matters;
- Correspond with IRAS on its Corporate Income Tax matters through any mode of communication including telephone calls, myTax Mails, emails, hardcopy letters and in-person meetings.

title: IRAS | e-Submission of Commission
text:
On this page:

**NEW!**

From the Year of Assessment (YA) 2024, commission-paying intermediaries are required under the law to collect, retain and submit the identification and income information of their commission agents if they receive notice(s) from IRAS informing them to do so.

## What you need to do if you receive such notice(s) from IRAS

With effect from YA 2024, commission-paying organisations that receive the following notices from IRAS must comply with the following:

- If your organisation received the “Notice to Collect and Retain Identification Information and Income Information”, the organisation must collect and retain the identification information, as well as the income information of commission agents who are not your employees from the effective date stated in the Notice; and
- If your organisation received the “Notice to Submit Identification Information and Income Information”, the organisation must submit the required information to IRAS between **2 Jan and 1 Mar** in the specified year.

Check the [Commission-Paying Organisation Search](https://mytax.iras.gov.sg/portal/auto-inclusion/search/commission) to find out if your organisation is registered under the e-Submission of Commission and is required to submit the identification and income information of their commission agents to IRAS.

Expand all

[For EXISTING organisations registered under the e-Submission of Commission](https://www.iras.gov.sg/digital-services/commission-paying-organisations/e-submission-of-commission#for-existing-organisations-registered-under-the-e-submission-of-commission)

If your organisation submitted commission income information to IRAS in the past, it is likely to already be registered under the e-Submission of Commission, and will receive the abovementioned notices from IRAS. If your organisation receives the “Notice to Submit Identification Information and Income Information”, your organisation is required to submit the information to IRAS for YA 2026 (for in

title: IRAS | e-Submission of Commission
text:
s from IRAS. If your organisation receives the “Notice to Submit Identification Information and Income Information”, your organisation is required to submit the information to IRAS for YA 2026 (for income earned in 2025).

If your organisation receives such notice(s) from IRAS but **did not make any payments** to commission agents in the preceding year and/ or will not be making such payments in the future, please inform IRAS by completing the [declaration form](https://go.gov.sg/iras-commission-declaration) **by 1 Mar**.

[For NEW organisations not registered under the e-Submission of Commission](https://www.iras.gov.sg/digital-services/commission-paying-organisations/e-submission-of-commission#for-new-organisations-not-registered-under-the-e-submission-of-commission)

Organisations that are not registered under the e-Submission of Commission will be onboarded from YA 2026 (income earned from 2025 onwards). When your organisation receives the “Notice to Collect and Retain Identification Information and Income
Information”, your organisation will be required to register for e-Submission of Commission and provide additional information by completing the [registration form](https://go.gov.sg/iras-commission) **by 30 September**.

If your organisation receives the “Notice to Collect and Retain Identification Information and Income Information”, **but will not be making payment** to commission agents, please inform us by completing
the [online form](https://go.gov.sg/iras-commission) **by 30 September**.

Please note that your organisation is still required to inform us by submitting
a new [registration form](https://go.gov.sg/iras-commission) if your organisation makes such payment(s) in the future.

If your organisation **made payment to commission agents** who are not your employees **but did not receive any notices** from IRAS, you will need to register for e-Submission of Commission by completing
the [registration form](https://go.gov.sg/iras-commission). We will a

title: IRAS | e-Submission of Commission
text:
ur employees **but did not receive any notices** from IRAS, you will need to register for e-Submission of Commission by completing
the [registration form](https://go.gov.sg/iras-commission). We will acknowledge your registration via email and send you notices to submit the information electronically to IRAS.

## Who to collect, retain and submit information for

Commission-paying intermediaries must collect, retain and submit income information for:

- Commission agents who are not your employees
- Overseas commission agents^ who carried out work or has permanent establishment\* in Singapore

IRAS will pre-fill the self-employed commission agents’ Income Tax Returns with the submitted information.

\* Permanent establishment refers to a fixed place of business through which the business of an enterprise is wholly or partly carried on and normally includes a place of management, a branch, an office, etc.

^ Overseas commission agent may suffer double taxation when the same income is being taxed twice - once in Singapore where the income arises and another time in the overseas tax jurisdiction where the income is received. However, if the overseas agent is a tax resident of a foreign jurisdiction that has concluded an [Avoidance of Double Taxation Agreement (DTA)](https://www.iras.gov.sg/taxes/international-tax/list-of-dtas-limited-dtas-and-eoi-arrangements) with Singapore, he/ she may be protected from being taxed twice on the same income in Singapore, depending on the provisions of the DTA. You may refer to the [IRAS e-Tax Guide on Avoidance of Double Taxation Agreements (DTAs)](https://www.iras.gov.sg/docs/default-source/archive/etaxguide_income-tax_avoidance-of-double-taxation-agreements-(dtas)_3rd-edition.pdf?sfvrsn=5b9f3b30_17) (PDF, 258KB) to learn more about the DTA.

## Learn about e-Submission of Commission

- [Introduction to e-Submission of Commission](https://www.iras.gov.sg/docs/default-source/uploadedfiles/pdf/introduction-to-e-submission-of-commission.

- [Information to prepare for commission submission](https://www.iras.gov.sg/digital-services/commission-paying-organisations/e-submission-of-commission/prepare-information-for-submission)

## Submit commission income records

The due date to submit the commission income information is **1 Mar** every year. Organisations can start submitting from 2 Jan. We strongly encourage you to submit the records early to avoid last minute rush.

For more information on submission and amendment of commission income records, please refer to:

- [User guide on authorisation for submission of commission income records via Corppass and update contact details via myTax Portal](https://www.iras.gov.sg/docs/default-source/uploadedfiles/pdf/corppass-authorisation-user-guide_commission.pdf?sfvrsn=29ad4657_13) (PDF, 1,004KB)
- [Submit commission income records](https://www.iras.gov.sg/digital-services/commission-paying-organisations/e-submission-of-commission/submit-commission-records)
- [Amend submitted commission income records](https://www.iras.gov.sg/digital-services/commission-paying-organisations/e-submission-of-commission/amend-submitted-commission-income-records)

## FAQs

For other details on e-Submission of Commission, please refer to our [FAQs](https://www.iras.gov.sg/docs/default-source/uploadedfiles/pdf/faq-on-e-submission-of-commission.pdf?sfvrsn=d5539752_23 "FAQs") (PDF, 353KB).

text:
The e-stamping Portal is integrated with [myTax Portal](https://mytax.iras.gov.sg/). To log in, select "Stamp Duty" and log in as "Individual User" or "Business User". For business users who are stamping their documents, you must register for a Stamp Duty Account ID (SDID) via myTax Portal (Stamp Duty Login). To register for a new SDID, please assign "Corporate" role through Corppass to access the portal. An SDID will be created when you have successfully registered for your corporate Stamp Duty Account. For individual users to perform stamping, please log in via Singpass.
